preview_text:
The model rates this match as nearly even, with Germany favored slightly at 36% to Ecuador's 33%. The gap between the two sides amounts to just 3 percentage points in win probability, suggesting a highly competitive contest where either team has a credible path to victory.

The draw emerges as a plausible outcome at 32%, nearly as likely as an Ecuador win. The W/D/L distribution is relatively balanced across all three results, indicating genuine uncertainty about the match outcome. No single scenario dominates the forecast, pointing toward a tightly contested encounter where the margin of victory—or stalemate—remains genuinely open.

The market and Polymarket prices diverge meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks assign Germany a 55% win probability, some 19 percentage points higher than the model's 36%, while assigning Ecuador only 20%—13 points below the model. The draw contracts to 25% in market pricing. This gap suggests the market views Germany as a stronger favorite than the statistical model does. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is correct; the discrepancy is material and warrants awareness.
